samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>A pipelined unsigned 8bit multiplier.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling the multiplication process.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="N" type="integer">Number of bits in the multiplier and multiplicand.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7:0]" width_description="[15:0]">Array of registers to hold temporary values during multiplication.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3:0]" width_description="[15:0]">Array of registers to hold intermediate sums.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product result.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Registering inputs
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_en_out_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_en_out_reg[N-1:0] <= {mul_en_in, mul_en_out_reg[N-1:1]};
            end
        end
        
        // Assigning outputs
        assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[N];
        
        // Generating partial products
        genvar i;
        generate
            for (i = 0; i < N; i = i + 1) begin : gen_partial_products
                assign temp[i][7:0] = mul_b[i] ? mul_a << i : 0;
            end
        endgenerate
        // Adding partial products together
        integer j;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                for (j = 0; j < N; j = j + 1) begin
                    sum[j] <= 0;
                end
            end else begin
                for (j = 0; j < N/2; j = j + 1) begin
                    sum[j] <= temp[j*2] + temp[j*2+1];
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Final addition stage
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[1];
            end
        end
        
        // Assigning final product output
        assign mul_out = mul_en_out_reg[N] ? mul_out_reg : 0;
    </implementation>
</module>
